Why estimates fail

Most estimates fail for reasons that have nothing to do with laziness or incompetence, which is exactly why they keep failing after you tell people to "try harder." Three forces do most of the damage, and understanding them changes how you read every number your team gives you.

workflowProgress.resultPreview:
Why estimates fail

An estimate is a probabilistic guess about the future made with incomplete information. That definition already contains most of the reasons estimates go wrong, but three failures do the heavy lifting, and you will watch all three play out on your team within your first month.

workflowProgress.resultPreview:
Why estimates fail

The first thing to understand about a bad estimate is that it usually wasn't a lie or even carelessness. It was a sincere prediction made by someone who wanted to be helpful, and it was wrong for reasons that were baked in before the number left their mouth.
